label: Free discovery to upgrade
persona: Visitor or tennis player getting oriented
painPoint: A visitor should not have to guess where tennis context lives or what unlocks next.
accessRule: Free users can explore public tennis intelligence and contribute Data Assist uploads without seeing private workspace controls.
accessCheck: Signed-out or Free access can use public discovery and Data Assist entry without private workspace controls.
evidenceArtifact: Public discovery proof cue, public detail screenshot, Data Assist review-first handoff cue, Data Assist upload state proof cue, review-state screenshot, and pricing handoff screenshot.
returnStateProof: Return to Explore or Pricing and confirm the user still sees useful public context plus a clear next tier.

steps.action: Search public players, teams, leagues, rankings, or areas.
steps.productSignal: Useful public tennis context appears before any upgrade pitch.

steps.action: Open Data Assist when public context is stale.
steps.productSignal: Upload language makes review status clear instead of implying instant trusted data.
steps.evidence: Data Assist review-first handoff cue, upload state proof cue, and upload/review copy.

steps.action: Compare plans only after seeing what the free surface can do.
steps.productSignal: Upgrade path names the right next workspace.

handoffs.label: Unlock Player when public context should become personal.
handoffs.trigger: Visitor wants My Lab, matchup prep, follows, or a player-linked home.
handoffs.proof: Pricing handoff points to Player without hiding free value.

label: Player practice progress loop
persona: Player using TenAceIQ during and between practice sessions
painPoint: Players need a simple way to turn lessons, goals, and practice time into visible progress.
accessRule: Player access unlocks personal spaces; invited players can use coach-linked assigned work where the coach tier provides the relationship.
accessCheck: Player access opens My Lab and Level Up; coach-linked assigned work is visible only when the relationship exists.
evidenceArtifact: Mobile active-task screenshot, Level Up local sync proof cue, saved 0-5 proof screenshot, next-action screenshot, My Lab Level Up return-state panel, and My Lab refresh proof cue.
returnStateProof: Refresh Level Up and My Lab, then confirm the Level Up return-state panel and My Lab refresh proof cue pull recent proof forward with honest local/synced copy.

steps.action: Choose one Level Up focus based on identity, coach assignment, or current goal.
steps.productSignal: Recommended lane, module, or challenge explains why it fits the player.

steps.action: Start the drill, exercise, or routine with prior selections collapsed.
steps.productSignal: The active task is the main screen on phone with clear do/track/score guidance.

steps.action: Rate proof 0-5 and add one tiny note only if it changes the next practice.
steps.productSignal: Numbers come first and the note stays lightweight.
steps.evidence: Saved rating, note state, and Level Up local sync proof cue.

steps.action: Return later to see progress and the next useful rep.
steps.productSignal: The Level Up return-state panel pulls recent work and next action forward without a generic empty dashboard.
steps.evidence: My Lab Level Up return-state panel and My Lab refresh proof cue after refresh.

handoffs.label: Share proof with coach when linked.
handoffs.trigger: Player completes coach-assigned work or chooses to share a recap.
handoffs.proof: Coach can find the same proof signal from the student context.

label: Coach assignment to lesson loop
persona: Coach managing a student between private lessons
painPoint: Coach and player alignment should continue between lessons without long journals or disconnected homework.
accessRule: Coach tier can invite linked players, assign tools, review proof, and plan the next session.
accessCheck: Coach can invite, assign, and review only linked students; player sees only their assigned challenge and own proof.
evidenceArtifact: Invite/link state, Linking proof privacy cue, Invite acceptance proof cue, Coach invite account proof cue, assignment card, player proof completion, coach review proof sync cue, Coach lesson support proof cue, and Level Up assignment handoff cue.
returnStateProof: Return to Coach Hub and coach planner, then confirm the latest proof informs the next lesson focus.

steps.action: Send an invite link and confirm the player links to the coach.
steps.productSignal: The relationship is clear to both coach and player.
steps.evidence: Linked player state, Invite acceptance proof cue, and Coach invite account proof cue.

steps.action: Assign one tool that supports the current tennis habit.
steps.productSignal: Assignment is specific, not extra work for its own sake.

steps.action: Review the player's proof and quick note.
steps.productSignal: Coach can see score, note, due state, next implication, and whether proof is synced into the review queue.

steps.action: Convert proof/readiness into a one-hour lesson plan.
steps.productSignal: Lesson blocks match identity, readiness, and recent assigned work.
steps.evidence: Coach lesson support proof cue, Level Up assignment handoff cue, and lesson plan handoff.

handoffs.label: Player receives the assigned challenge inside Level Up.
handoffs.trigger: Coach assigns a card or module.
handoffs.proof: Player sees challenge and can complete proof.

label: Captain week decision loop
persona: Team captain setting a weekly lineup
painPoint: Captains need to make lineup and communication decisions without rebuilding context in scattered tools.
accessRule: Captain tier includes Player value plus team-week tools for availability, scouting, lineup, and communication.
accessCheck: Captain access includes team-week tools and Player value without exposing league coordinator controls.
evidenceArtifact: Availability/readiness state, lineup option, save-status local honesty cue, Captain local sync proof cue, Captain decision handoff proof cue, Compete Bridge captain handoff cue, opponent/team context, and team brief screenshot.
returnStateProof: Return after a result or fixture update and confirm the next week starts from current team context without implying browser-saved work is account sync.

steps.action: Review availability, readiness, opponent context, and roster options.
steps.productSignal: The week opens with the decision inputs visible and save status explains what carries forward.
steps.evidence: Availability/readiness state, save-status local honesty cue, Captain local sync proof cue, and Captain decision handoff proof cue.

steps.action: Build and compare lineup options.
steps.productSignal: Scenario comparison helps choose instead of just listing players.

steps.action: Send or prepare the team-facing brief.
steps.productSignal: The captain can close the loop with the team.

steps.action: Return after results and prepare next week.
steps.productSignal: Results and team context feed the next decision.
steps.evidence: Compete Bridge captain handoff cue and result/context handoff.

handoffs.label: League result context informs captain decisions.
handoffs.trigger: Schedules or results are updated by the league/coordinator flow.
handoffs.proof: Captain can see the public/member-facing context and Compete Bridge captain handoff cue that affects the week.

label: League operation to visibility loop
persona: League coordinator running a season, ladder, or tournament
painPoint: Coordinators need one operating workspace for schedules, results, standings, and visibility.
accessRule: League tier gives coordinator tools while public/member league pages expose only the intended context.
accessCheck: Coordinator can manage league operations while public/member pages hide private controls.
evidenceArtifact: Coordinator schedule/result state, League Office operation proof cue, source-to-public proof cue, standings update, public league page, and privacy check screenshot.
returnStateProof: Return to League Office and public league detail after result entry to confirm the same match-week reality appears.

steps.action: Set or review league structure, participants, and schedule.
steps.productSignal: The coordinator can manage structure without spreadsheet workarounds.

steps.action: Record or review results from safe fixture data.
steps.productSignal: Result state updates the intended source of truth.

steps.action: Check public/member league context.
steps.productSignal: Members can see schedule/result/standing context without private controls.
steps.evidence: League Office operation proof cue, source-to-public proof cue, public league screen, and privacy check.

steps.action: Resolve exceptions and keep the next week moving.
steps.productSignal: Exceptions are visible and actionable.

handoffs.label: League context feeds captain team-week planning.
handoffs.trigger: Schedule, result, or standings context changes.
handoffs.proof: Captain-facing surfaces reflect the same match-week reality.

label: Full-Court role switching loop
persona: Multi-role operator using several TenAceIQ workspaces
painPoint: Multi-role users need clean access across every workspace without tier confusion.
accessRule: Full-Court users should move through Player, Coach, Captain, and League surfaces without stale locks or duplicate upgrade prompts.
accessCheck: Full-Court access opens Player, Coach, Captain, and League surfaces with no stale paid locks.
evidenceArtifact: Pricing tier copy, Full-Court access pass cue, Full-Court tool fit proof cue, Full-Court role switching proof cue, plus one access screenshot from each paid tool.
returnStateProof: Return through navigation and confirm role switching remains clear without duplicate upgrade prompts.

steps.action: Confirm Full-Court tier language and access state.
steps.productSignal: Full-Court explains every included tool.

steps.action: Open Player, Coach, Captain, and League surfaces.
steps.productSignal: No paid tool shows stale locks or redundant upgrade prompts.

steps.action: Come back and choose the right tool for the need.
steps.productSignal: Role switching feels deliberate and understandable.
steps.evidence: Full-Court access pass cue, Full-Court tool fit proof cue, and Full-Court role switching proof cue.

handoffs.label: Use Coach when the job is player development.
handoffs.trigger: Operator needs assignment, proof review, or lesson planning.
handoffs.proof: Coach workspace opens without gating confusion.

handoffs.label: Use Captain when the job is team-week decisions.
handoffs.trigger: Operator needs lineup, readiness, scouting, or team messages.
handoffs.proof: Captain workspace opens without gating confusion.

label: Admin access and data quality loop
persona: Internal operator protecting access and tennis data quality
painPoint: Internal users need to keep access and tennis intelligence trustworthy without creating product drift.
accessRule: Admin/Internal access is protected and should only touch safe fixtures during test week.
accessCheck: Admin-only tools are protected, use safe fixtures, and do not expose private controls to non-admin tiers.
evidenceArtifact: Access repair before/after screenshot, fixture safety rollback cue, Admin access repair proof cue, import queue data trust guard cue, Admin import outcome proof cue, and affected surface confirmation.
returnStateProof: Return to the affected product surface and confirm access or data quality changed only where intended.

steps.action: Review a test account access issue.
steps.productSignal: Starting tier, target tier, and affected surfaces are clear.
steps.evidence: Access repair before/after, fixture safety rollback cue, and Admin access repair proof cue.

steps.action: Review imported tennis data before it becomes trusted context.
steps.productSignal: Fixture data stays isolated and unreviewed data is not treated as trusted.
steps.evidence: Import review state, data trust guard cue, and Admin import outcome proof cue.

steps.action: Confirm affected product surfaces after repair/review.
steps.productSignal: Access or data changes show where intended without creating tier drift.

handoffs.label: Data Assist submissions move into review.
handoffs.trigger: A user uploads scorecard, schedule, or roster data.
handoffs.proof: Admin queue shows review state before product intelligence changes.
